question_text: A light bulb transfers 100 J of electrical energy into 15 J of light energy and 85 J of heat energy. Which statement best explains what happens to the energy?

options.label: A
options.text: 85 J of energy is destroyed because it is wasted as heat

options.label: B
options.text: All 100 J of energy is transferred but only 15 J is useful; the other 85 J becomes heat

options.label: C
options.text: The light bulb creates 15 J of light energy from nothing

options.label: D
options.text: Energy is lost because 100 J does not equal 15 J plus 85 J

explanation: Energy cannot be created or destroyed—it can only be transferred from one form to another. The light bulb transfers all 100 J of electrical energy into light and heat. The light is useful, but the heat is wasted energy because we don't want it. Both forms of energy still exist; they're just in different forms.
